prerequisites: A running MongoDB instance reachable from the connector, configured as one of the supported instance types: Standalone, Replica Set, or MongoDB Atlas cluster.
A target database name to write to (the required `database` config field). MongoDB database naming restrictions apply.
A MongoDB user with permission to create collections and write documents to the target database. The `readWrite` built-in role on the target database grants this access.
Access control enabled on self-managed deployments (mongod started with `--auth` or `security.authorization`) so the user can authenticate.
For connecting through a bastion host: an intermediate SSH server with direct network access to the MongoDB instance, plus either an SSH private key or an SSH user password.

authMethods.name: Username and password (SCRAM)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to MongoDB with a username and password via the `auth_type` config and the secret `auth_type.password` field. SCRAM (Salted Challenge Response Authentication Mechanism) is MongoDB's default authentication mechanism and uses username and password credentials. The authenticating user must hold a role such as `readWrite` on the target database.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (optional)
authMethods.summary: Optionally tunnel the connection through a bastion host using the `tunnel_method` config. Choose SSH Key Authentication, which uses the secret `tunnel_method.ssh_key` (an RSA private key), or Password Authentication, which uses the secret `tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`. Selecting No Tunnel connects directly without these credentials.

setupSteps.title: Choose and prepare the MongoDB instance
setupSteps.body: Select the `instance_type` that matches your deployment: Standalone (host and port), Replica Set (member server addresses and replica set name), or MongoDB Atlas (cluster URL). Ensure the instance is network-reachable from the connector.

setupSteps.title: Enable access control and create a write-capable user
setupSteps.body: On self-managed deployments, enable authentication (start mongod with `--auth` or set `security.authorization`). Create a dedicated MongoDB user and grant it a role that allows creating collections and writing documents to the target database, for example the built-in `readWrite` role scoped to that database.

setupSteps.title: Provide authentication credentials
setupSteps.body: Set `auth_type` to username/password authentication and supply the username and the secret `auth_type.password`. MongoDB uses SCRAM by default to verify these credentials.

setupSteps.title: Specify the target database
setupSteps.body: Enter the required `database` field with the name of the database the connector should write to. It may be an existing database or one to be created, subject to MongoDB naming restrictions.

setupSteps.title: Configure the SSH tunnel if required
setupSteps.body: If MongoDB is only reachable through a bastion, set `tunnel_method` to SSH Key Authentication and provide the secret `tunnel_method.ssh_key`, or to Password Authentication and provide the secret `tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password`. Otherwise leave it as No Tunnel for a direct connection.
